channel.title: Our News Bahamas Podcast (Edited)
channel.description:
This is an unofficial version of the Our News podcast edited to be more consise. All rights are reserved to Our News Bahamas and Cable Bahamas Ltd. You can find the original podcast here on Apple Podcasts.

Our News, your daily one-hour passport to the latest updates and happenings. From breaking news to in-depth features, join us as we navigate through the headlines, providing you with a comprehensive view of the day's events. Stay informed, stay connected – Our News is your trusted source for the pulse of The Bahamas, delivered every day. Visit https://www.ournews.bs for more Bahamas news any time.
channel.copyright: Copyright ©2024 Our News Bahamas. All rights reserved.

item.title: Eight-Year-Old Boy Airlifted to Florida After Suffering Serious Head Injuries in North Bimini Shooting — Sunday, May 24th, 2026
item.itunes:title: Eight-Year-Old Boy Airlifted to Florida After Suffering Serious Head Injuries in North Bimini Shooting — Sunday, May 24th, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News Weekend, a community is in shock after an eight-year-old boy is airlifted for emergency treatment following a tragic shooting in North Bimini. We dive into the political fallout as a former House Speaker reveals what he believes triggered a crushing election defeat and calls for major electoral reform. Our team also follows the US Ambassador’s visit to BTVI to expand student opportunities and takes you under the waves for the unveiling of Grand Bahama’s first underwater sculpture museum. Watch the full broadcast for these stories, a record-shattering performance from Devyn Charlton, and a special look at the mental health legacy of Hurricane Dorian. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.

item.title: Body of Missing 56-Year-Old Barbara Lewis Discovered in Grand Bahama Following Intensive Search — Monday, May 25th, 2026
item.itunes:title: Body of Missing 56-Year-Old Barbara Lewis Discovered in Grand Bahama Following Intensive Search — Monday, May 25th, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News, a community in Grand Bahama is left searching for answers after the tragic discovery of a missing woman's body, while police in Eleuthera investigate a separate death involving a man found in coastal waters. We bring you the latest developments on the eight-year-old shooting victim receiving emergency treatment in the US and the national security minister’s stern warning on firearm safety. Our team also looks into the possibility of a new "conch season" following a troubling report on overfishing and details a fresh police alert regarding fraudulent government payment scams. Stay tuned for the full broadcast to catch an inspiring story of youth outreach in our high schools and your complete national weather forecast. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: Nurse union, Ebola, no-bid contracts
item.itunes:title: 12-Year-Old Boy and 40-Year-Old Man Arraigned in Court Following Tragic Shooting of Eight-Year-Old in Bimini — Thursday, May 28th, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News, legal proceedings begin following a tragic shooting in Bimini as a 12-year-old boy and a 40-year-old man face a magistrate’s court in connection with the incident. We investigate the growing debate over the latest national budget, from the Nurses Union’s concerns about crumbling clinics to Family Island residents questioning the timeline for promised fire equipment. Our team also brings you an update on the Ebola monitoring situation at Princess Margaret Hospital and the Finance Minister’s defense of controversial government contract practices. Join us for the full broadcast for these headlines, highlights from the Best of the Best music festival in Miami, and your complete weekend weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: Murder Escape, Politician, Nurses Budget
item.itunes:title: FNM Leader Michael Pintard Demands Transparency Over Allegations Linking High-Ranking Politician to US Drug Trafficking Investigation — Saturday, May 30th, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News Weekend, political tensions boil over as the opposition demands answers regarding a high-profile drug trafficking investigation while a captured murder suspect faces sentencing after months on the run. We examine the latest concerns from the Nurses Union over the national budget and highlight new initiatives aimed at helping young Bahamians achieve the dream of home ownership. Our team also follows a major wave of corporate giving across the islands and takes you inside a star-studded performance at Atlantis by a three-time Grammy winner. Catch the full broadcast for these headlines, a complete sports wrap-up, and more of the news you need to know. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: Labor Day, Mechanic Shop, PACE
item.itunes:title: Labour Week Activities Officially Launch with Historic Change to Annual Parade Route — Sunday, May 31st, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News Weekend, Labour Week activities officially kick off with a historic change to the annual parade route while frustrated residents in a New Providence community plead for intervention over a neighbourhood mechanic shop. We celebrate the newest class of RBDF Rangers in Grand Bahama and take an emotional look at the PACE Foundation’s efforts to support teen mothers facing a cycle of abuse and trauma. Stay tuned for a critical update as the 2026 hurricane season begins, along with national soccer championship highlights and your complete holiday weekend weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: Halkitis Resignation Calls, Davis Silent
item.itunes:title: PM Maintains Silence on Bahamian Detained in U.S. Drug Case and Alleged Political Ties — Monday, June 1st, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News, Prime Minister Philip Davis remains silent as questions intensify regarding a Bahamian man detained in the United States and his alleged connections to a local politician. We examine the growing calls for the Finance Minister’s resignation and provide essential preparedness updates as the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season officially gets underway. Our team also takes you inside the Caribbean Development Bank’s annual meeting at Baha Mar and brings you the latest on a tragic discovery in a western gated community. Stay tuned for these stories and a heartwarming community effort to support special needs education. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: Labor Harmony, Ebola Clear, Body Found
item.itunes:title: Union Leaders Divided Over Ethics of Publicly Supporting Political Parties During Labor Week — Tuesday, June 2nd, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News, tensions rise during Labor Week as union leaders debate the ethics of political endorsements while government officials address ongoing industrial concerns. We have the latest results from a recent Ebola monitoring situation and a full report on a tragic homicide that has rocked a quiet western neighborhood. Our team also follows the chilling new developments in the disappearance of an American woman in Abaco and a significant court victory for a man injured in police custody. Join us for these stories, plus a major cash seizure investigation and your complete tropical weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: NIB Benefits Up, Budget Debate, CDB
item.itunes:title: NIB Beneficiaries to Receive Boost in Benefits Starting July 1st — Wednesday, June 3rd, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News, National Insurance beneficiaries prepare for a boost in payments as new adjustments take effect this July, while a top financial expert raises critical questions about the government's projected budget surplus. We go inside the high-stakes opening of the Caribbean Development Bank’s annual meeting at Baha Mar and bring you Finance Minister Michael Halkitis’ direct response to intensifying resignation rumors. Our team also investigates a tragic fatal crash on Fire Trail Road and clears the air on viral reports of a local principal being attacked. Stay tuned for the latest on a historic sentencing in an attempted murder case, a major hot streak for a Bahamian baseball star, and your complete tropical weather outlook as unsettled conditions persist. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.

item.title: New Ministries & MP Accusations
item.itunes:title: Davis Administration Defends Largest Cabinet in History Following Creation of New Government Ministries — Thursday, June 4th, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News, the Davis administration faces intense scrutiny over the launch of several new government ministries and a controversial proposed amendment to the Bahamas Nationality Act. We bring you the latest on a major leadership shakeup within the Free National Movement as the party chairman confirms he’s stepping down, plus details on a high-ranking officer granted bail in a murder case. Our team also investigates the ongoing banking frustrations of local customers and a desperate plea for land to support the country's homeless populations. Stay tuned for your full Labor Day parade update and a complete holiday weekend weather outlook to keep you prepared for the days ahead. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: Labor Day and Abaco Investigation
item.itunes:title: Thousands March in Solidarity for Annual Sir Randol Fawkes Labor Day Parade — Friday, June 5th, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News, thousands take to the streets for a historic Labor Day celebration as union leaders reflect on the legacy of Sir Randol Fawkes and the ongoing struggle for workers' rights. We provide a major update on the international search in Abaco for a missing American woman as the investigation shifts from a missing persons case to a suspected murder. Our team also investigates a string of recent violence in the capital and examines the "brain drain" crisis discussed at the Caribbean Development Bank’s annual meeting. Plus, stay tuned for your complete holiday weekend weather forecast and a recap of the top plays from the world of sports. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: Shootings, Labor Day, Teacher Union
item.itunes:title: Young Father Gunned Down Near His Home in Bain Town Shooting — Saturday, June 6th, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News Weekend, communities in New Providence and Grand Bahama are reeling following a series of fatal shootings that have left two families in mourning. Tensions reach a boiling point in the education sector as the Bahamas Union of Teachers responds to a "shot over the bow" regarding government salary promises, while immigration officials ramp up enforcement with dozens of deportations. We also join the nation in remembering the victims of a tragic holiday accident and provide an exclusive look at the growing partnerships surrounding the multi-million dollar Royal Beach Club project. Plus, stay tuned for highlights from the baseball diamond and your complete weekend weather forecast as the first week of hurricane season continues. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: Triple shooting, CDB interview, labor
item.itunes:title: One Dead and Two Hospitalized in Brazen Daylight Triple Shooting on Constitution Drive — Sunday, June 7th, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News Weekend, a violent holiday weekend concludes with a brazen triple shooting on Constitution Drive as police investigate a series of murders and a suspicious death across three islands. We bring you an exclusive sit-down with the President of the Caribbean Development Bank on the region's battle for hurricane resilience and explore historic labor law reforms aimed at protecting the well-being of Bahamian workers. Our team also introduces you to a dedicated group of "Police Legends" caring for retired, shut-in colleagues and captures the vibrant energy of the Bahamas Masqueraders’ 11th-anniversary road march. Stay tuned for these headlines, a full weekend sports wrap-up, and your seven-day weather outlook to start your week. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.

item.itunes:title: FM Halkidis Controversy; Violent Weekend
item.description: Tonight on Our News, the political showdown intensifies as the opposition demands the resignation of Finance Minister Michael Halkitis over controversial past associations, sparking a face-off between rival supporters in Parliament Square. We examine the critical recruitment challenges facing the Department of Corrections as officials struggle to find qualified male candidates to fill their ranks. Our team also looks into the sweeping reforms promised at the Public Parks and Public Beaches Authority following reports of massive overspending, and we bring you the latest on a string of weekend violence that has left families across the country in mourning. item.itunes:title: Derelict vehicles, BDOC, tourists arrested
item.description: Tonight on Our News, the government launches an intensified crackdown on derelict vehicles and illegal dumping, introducing new spot fines and land acquisition to clean up local communities. Controversy brews as a top law enforcement official faces backlash over remarks regarding the recruitment of male and female officers, while a man is hauled before the courts for an alleged high-priced scam using a forged cabinet minister's letter. Our team also brings you the details on a chaotic downtown brawl involving American tourists that left multiple officers injured and examines the regional ripple effects of a massive 6.1 magnitude earthquake. item.description: Tonight on Our News, the Department of Corrections Commissioner issues a formal apology following a public outcry over comments regarding female recruits, while a high-stakes showdown erupts in the House of Assembly over an attempt to table a controversial US affidavit. Tensions continue to boil in Parliament as the House Speaker stands her ground in a fiery clash with the opposition over the Public Accounts Committee’s mandate and the future of government oversight. We also bring you the latest on a tragic snorkeling accident off West Bay Street and the government's defense of the local egg industry against rising international competition. item.title: Power, Roads, Pre-clearance & More
item.itunes:title: Power, Roads, Pre-clearance & More
item.description: Tonight on Our News Weekend, Energy Minister JoBeth Coleby-Davis alerts residents to prepare for potential summer power interruptions as BPL undergoes significant infrastructure upgrades across New Providence. We provide the latest details on a tragic marine accident in Abaco that has claimed the life of a teenager and follow a high-profile visit from the U.S. Ambassador regarding the potential return of pre-clearance services to Grand Bahama. Our team also explores a major employment push at a local job fair and speaks with experts on recognizing the signs of "functional depression" following a recent rise in suspected suicides. Join us for these headlines, a full wrap-up from the sports world, and your complete tropical weather outlook to start your week. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.itunes:title: Junkanoo Fire, Nursing Scandal, Minister Backed
item.description: Tonight on Our News Weekend, investigators are on the scene at Junkanoo Beach following a late-night structural fire at the popular destination, while Progressive Liberal Party MPs rally behind Finance Minister Michael Halkitis amid growing calls for his resignation. We dive into the controversy surrounding a Grand Bahama nursing program as students and administrators speak out, and bring you the details of a major drug crackdown resulting in multiple arrests across the northern islands. Our team also examines the potential threat of international tariffs on the local fishing industry and takes you to the heart of the 37th annual Pineapple Festival in Eleuthera. Plus, stay tuned for the latest highlights from the sports world and a full tropical weather outlook as a new moisture plume approaches the islands. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.description: Tonight on Our News, Finance Minister Michael Halkitis responds to the mounting pressure on Bahamian households as gas prices hit a staggering seven dollars a gallon at the pump. item.title: Traffic Reforms, Outages & Dad
item.itunes:title: Traffic Reforms, Outages & Dad
item.description: OUR NEWS: Transport Minister Outlines New Vehicle Licensing Fees and Road Traffic Modernization Plan — Saturday, June 20th, 2026 Tonight on Our News Weekend, the government unveils a major modernization of the Road Traffic Department featuring a new tiered fee structure and a transition to a cashless payment system. We also head to Eleuthera, where residents are voicing their frustrations over "unbearable" power outages that have left the island in darkness for nearly 24 hours. A former House clerk joins us to explain the historic rules of parliamentary privilege amidst ongoing legislative tension, while the Ministry of Culture reveals the symbolic new logo for the nation’s upcoming 53rd Independence celebrations. Plus, stay tuned for a special Father’s Day look at the art of making the perfect crab and rice, a vital community blood drive in Grand Bahama, and your complete holiday weekend sports and weather wrap-up. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: Jet Ski Dispute & Social Media Suicide
item.itunes:title: Jet Ski Dispute & Social Media Suicide
item.description:
Tonight on Our News Weekend, jet ski operators at Goodman’s Bay demand government action on long-promised infrastructure improvements while navigating the fallout of new international safety alerts. Our team also explores the complex link between social media and youth mental health with expert advice for parents on managing digital disconnect this summer. We follow a Bahamian trailblazer’s historic run for the Florida Senate and celebrate the latest graduates of a major community empowerment initiative. Plus, we share heartfelt tributes to the nation's fathers and provide your complete forecast as Saharan dust and rising temperatures take hold.

----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.

item.title: Power outages, fuel costs, Abaco shooting
item.itunes:title: Power outages, fuel costs, Abaco shooting
item.description: Tonight on Our News, escalating power outages and load shedding leave residents across several islands frustrated as summer temperatures reach dangerous highs. We examine the mounting pressure on gas station retailers as fuel prices hover near $7 a gallon and get the Transport Minister's response to a shocking viral altercation between jitney drivers. Our team also brings you the latest on a deadly shooting in Abaco and a major $1.6 million investment in youth development for Grand Bahama. Join us for these headlines, a record-breaking wrap-up from the national track and field championships, and your complete tropical weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: AG challenges FNM; property tax push
item.itunes:title: AG challenges FNM; property tax push
item.description: Tonight on Our News, the Attorney General issues a bold challenge to the opposition over controversial cabinet appointments while a new voice in the Senate calls for strict accountability and measurable results in the national budget. We investigate the tragic details of a hit-and-run fatality on Grand Bahama and a harrowing shark attack involving a young visitor in the Exumas. Our team also looks into the push for property tax reform to protect homeowners and a major debate over televising parliamentary proceedings for greater transparency. Join us for these stories, a look at the latest international headlines, and your complete tropical weather and sports updates. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: Gardner Case, Coral Crisis, Power Woes
item.itunes:title: Gardner Case, Coral Crisis, Power Woes
item.description: Tonight on Our News, explosive new court documents in a high-profile U.S. drug case reveal alleged links between seized cash and local political campaign activity as a judge denies bail for a well-known figure. We take you to the OAS General Assembly as the Foreign Affairs Minister addresses regional stability, while scientists and government officials sound a dire warning over a fast-spreading disease threatening the nation's critical coral reefs. Our team also investigates a harrowing sexual assault involving an elderly resident and brings you the latest on the mounting frustration over persistent power outages gripping Western New Providence. Join us for these headlines, the launch of a multi-million dollar blue economy initiative, and a look at the "Love Our Locals" experience at Bahamar. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.itunes:title: Homicide, Shooting, Coral Reef
item.description: Tonight on Our News Weekend, a tragic fire investigation in Nassau Village takes a dark turn as police officially reclassify the case as a homicide following new autopsy results. We take you inside a regional summit focused on saving the nation’s coral reefs from a devastating disease and examine a major new plan to revitalize public parks and beaches nationwide. Our team also brings you the highlights from a spectacular Independence Day celebration at the U.S. Embassy and celebrates a legendary Bahamian sailor’s historic achievement on the water. Join us for these stories, an update on a local STEM camp for future innovators, and your complete weekend weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: 4 teens killed in crash; Parliament clash
item.itunes:title: 4 teens killed in crash; Parliament clash
item.description: Tonight on Our News, the nation is in mourning as families gather to identify four young women tragically killed in a horrific weekend car crash. Tensions boil over once again in the House of Assembly as the Opposition clashes with the Speaker over a high-profile international legal matter. We investigate a fatal double shooting in Chippingham and bring you the latest on a major infrastructure push for the country’s vocational training institutes. Join us for these stories, a vibrant display of culture at the annual pre-independence Beats Retreat, and your full holiday weekend sports and weather wrap-up. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: Protest, Pay Raise, Road Deaths
item.itunes:title: Protest, Pay Raise, Road Deaths
item.description: Tonight on Our News, tensions flare outside the Ministry of Works as hundreds of disgruntled employees take their grievances directly to the minister over controversial transfers and long-awaited promotions. A major union leader blasts proposed pay hikes for parliamentarians while educators remain in a standoff for their own agreements, and the nation grapples with a rising death toll on the streets following another fatal weekend crash. We break down the latest labor statistics showing a surge of women entering the workforce and bring you the details of a shocking court case involving a heinous crime against an elderly woman. Plus, catch the latest highlights from the sports world as a track icon returns to her winning ways and Team Bahamas gears up for a pivotal home-court showdown. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: Power Outage & CARICOM Summit
item.itunes:title: Power Outage & CARICOM Summit
item.description: Tonight on Our News Weekend, a sudden power disruption leaves homes and businesses across New Providence in the dark as officials investigate the cause of the widespread outage. Caribbean leaders, including a Bahamian delegation, gather in St. Lucia for the high-stakes CARICOM summit to tackle regional security and economic resilience. We also highlight the expansion of the nation's renewable energy efforts at the University of the Bahamas and bring you the latest on police operations in Bimini following a significant ammunition discovery. Plus, witness the dramatic moment victims chase down an armed robbery suspect and discover how a local summer camp is providing employment and mentorship for young Bahamians. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: Demolition, Blackouts & Shirley Crash
item.itunes:title: Demolition, Blackouts & Shirley Crash
item.description: Tonight on Our News, a government demolition exercise in an unregulated community turns physical as over 200 residents scramble to salvage their belongings from the rubble. We investigate a weekend of widespread power outages across the capital that impacted critical healthcare services and left thousands in the dark during a sweltering heatwave. Our team also brings you a somber update on the Shirley Street tragedy as the death toll rises and a 19-year-old faces multiple charges in court. Plus, we highlight a historic scientific discovery by a young Bahamian researcher and join regional leaders in St. Lucia for the opening of the 51st CARICOM Heads of Government meeting. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: Shirley Street Crash & Power Crisis
item.itunes:title: Shirley Street Crash & Power Crisis
item.description: Tonight on Our News, the grieving community of Cat Island comes together to prepare the final resting place for victims of the tragic Shirley Street car crash as a mother speaks out for the first time. We examine the impact of widespread power outages on critical hospital services and hear from the Energy Minister on the push for grid modernization. The Attorney General breaks down the legal complexities of vehicular manslaughter charges while residents scramble to secure backup power ahead of the peak hurricane season. Stay tuned for updates from the CARICOM summit in St. Lucia and highlights from the latest technical graduation ceremony. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: Bimini Fire, Caracal Dispute, Independence Countdown
item.itunes:title: Bimini Fire, Caracal Dispute, Independence Countdown
item.description: Tonight on Our News, a devastating blaze in North Bimini leaves families displaced and sparks urgent calls for improved emergency resources as the community rallies to fight the flames. We bring you the latest on a high-profile murder charge following the death of a well-known trade unionist and a touching tribute from the Junkanoo community to a victim of the Shirley Street tragedy. Our team also examines a major new road safety push aimed at curbing traffic fatalities and provides a preview of the final preparations for the nation's 53rd Independence celebrations. Plus, stay tuned for updates from the CARICOM summit in St. Lucia and your complete holiday weekend weather outlook as heat indices soar into the triple digits. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: Taxi Union Blockade, Bimini Fire, Tuberculosis Case
item.itunes:title: Taxi Union Blockade, Bimini Fire, Tuberculosis Case
item.description: Tonight on Our News, chaos erupts at Lynden Pindling International Airport as taxi drivers stage a major blockade to protest years of alleged unfair competition. We head to Bimini where a second devastating fire in just days has families pleading for better emergency resources, while health officials move to correct misinformation regarding tuberculosis cases in Eleuthera. The Energy Minister breaks her silence on rolling blackouts and soaring electricity bills as police investigate a fatal industrial accident at the national stadium. Plus, we bring you a major update on a high-profile attempted murder sentencing and your complete independence holiday weekend weather forecast. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.title: Plane Crash, Hit-and-Run, Independence Celebrations
item.itunes:title: Plane Crash, Hit-and-Run, Independence Celebrations
item.description: Tonight on Our News, a day of national celebration turns to heartbreak as the country mourns ten lives lost in a tragic plane crash in North Andros, leading to the immediate suspension of a local airline’s operating certificate. We bring you the latest on a fatal hit-and-run in Nassau Village and an active investigation into a reported bomb threat at a local resort. Our team captures the spirit of the 53rd Independence Day celebrations with highlights from the People’s Rush in Nassau and cultural events in Grand Bahama. Plus, we look at community giving from our NBA stars and provide your full holiday weekend weather forecast as extreme heat takes hold. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.description: Tonight on Our News, an experienced pilot breaks down public flight data from the tragic North Andros plane crash, suggesting mechanical failure may have played a larger role than pilot error. We bring you the details of another fatal traffic accident in Grand Bahama and follow the community-led relief efforts for families displaced by a devastating fire in Bimini. Frustration peaks as rolling blackouts continue to plague residents, with a government minister’s viral comments sparking fresh outrage over the nation's energy crisis. Plus, we join hundreds at a somber candlelight vigil for the Independence Day victims and showcase a local business dedicated to preserving authentic Bahamian-made crafts. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research.
